title: cuscuta australis subsp. breviflora
intro: Cuscuta australis subsp. breviflora, also known as dodder, is a parasitic plant species that grows by attaching itself to its host plants and drawing nutrients from them. Traditionally, Cuscuta australis subsp. breviflora has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of this plant are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and protect against oxidative stress. In traditional herbal preparations, Cuscuta australis subsp. breviflora is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures to support overall health and well-being.
